# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

# PostgreSQL Client Authentication Configuration File
# ===================================================
# This file controls: which hosts are allowed to connect, how clients
# authenticate themselves, which PostgreSQL user names they can use,
# which databases they can access. Records take one of these forms:
#
# local      DATABASE  USER  METHOD  [OPTIONS]
# host       DATABASE  USER  ADDRESS  METHOD  [OPTIONS]
# hostssl    DATABASE  USER  ADDRESS  METHOD  [OPTIONS]
# hostnossl  DATABASE  USER  ADDRESS  METHOD  [OPTIONS]
#
# DATABASE can be "all", "sameuser", "samerole", "replication", or a database name.
# USER can be "all", a user name, a group name prefixed with "+", or a system user name prefixed with "$".
# ADDRESS specifies the set of hosts the record matches. 
# METHOD can be "trust", "reject", "md5", "password", "gss", "sspi", "ident", "peer", "pam", "ldap", "radius", "cert", or "bsd".
#
# Options for METHOD can be provided in square brackets.

# Allow local connections for all users
local   all             all                                     peer

# Allow host-based connections for admin role
host    all             admin           192.168.1.0/24          md5

# Allow host-based connections for app role
host    all             app             192.168.1.0/24          md5

# Allow host-based connections for readonly role
host    all             readonly        192.168.1.0/24          md5

# Allow replication connections from localhost, by a user with the
# replication privilege.
local   replication     all                                     peer
host    replication     all             127.0.0.1/32            md5
host    replication     all             ::1/128                 md5
